title: Amazon and eBay Business Assistant (Lego Specialist / Online Arbitrage)
description:
Looking for a talented VA with past experience in working with US based Amazon selling. We are seeking a long-term employee who is good at problem solving. 



This is a Part-Time "Trial to Growth" position that will eventually require full-time as the business grows

This is a hybrid role: 50% Product Sourcing (Finding the Gold) and 50?ministrative/Operations (Managing the Machine).

You must be available for 3–4 Zoom calls per week and respond to messages within a few hours during working hours.





Key Responsibilities:



Specialized Sourcing: Use Tactical Arbitrage and manual "Storefront Stalking" to find Lego leads with high ROI and low competition.



Lead Validation: Use Keepa and SellerAmp to analyze price stability and "End of Life" (EOL) potential.



Data Management: Record all leads in Google Sheets with accurate profit calculations (including prep center fees).



eBay Support: Draft listings, edit photos (background removal), and manage customer inquiries for our eBay storefront.



Administrative: Monitor Amazon Account Health daily and assist with shipment reconciliation between our Prep Center and FBA.

 

Look out for and familiarize yourself with market trends in lego





Requirements:

English Fluency: C1 or C2 level (Written and Verbal). You must be able to communicate clearly with Amazon Support and US-based retailers.



Keepa & Tactical Arbitrage (REQUIRED): You must be able to read a Keepa chart like a pro—identifying price tanking, IP complaint patterns, and seasonal spikes.



SellerAmp (Required): Expert at calculating net profit after all fees.



Excel and Google Sheets (Required): Must be familiar and able to use google sheets and excel 



Lego Knowledge (Preferred but not required): Familiarity with Lego themes (Star Wars, Technic, Icons) and the retirement cycle as well as an enjoyment for legos



Communication: Strong written English for handling customer service on eBay and Amazon.



Internet: A stable, high-speed connection is a must.



Mindset: Analytical, proactive, and detail-oriented.





Benefits:



Flexible Schedule: Work whenever you are most productive, provided deadlines are met.



Bi-weekly Payment: Reliable, on-time payments.



Performance Bonuses: We believe in rewarding VAs who find "Deep Buy" leads that result in high-volume profit.



Growth: As the business grows, your role and compensation will grow with it.





How to Apply:

We value attention to detail above all else. To demonstrate that you have read this entire listing and can follow instructions perfectly:



Subject Line: Your application must start with your favorite animal followed by your full name.



Introduction: Tell us about a difficult time in your role as an Amazon VA and how you overcame it.



A brief summary of your experience with Keepa and Tactical Arbitrage.



Have you ever sourced Legos or toys? What is your favorite Lego theme to source and why?



Are you familiar with cross-listing products from Amazon to eBay?



Analysis Test: Briefly explain why we avoid listings where Amazon is a constant seller, even if the ROI looks good. Are there any exceptions where you would still recommend the buy?



Proof: Attach or link your English proficiency results and provide a brief video introduction of yourself.



Applications that do not follow the previous instructions will be automatically disqualified.



We will provide a 30-minute sample sourcing project to qualified candidates. 



Performance-Based Scalability: We are a fast-growing company. As you hit your KPIs (Key Performance Indicators) and help the business scale, your compensation will be reviewed to reflect your impact.



Look forward to hearing from you and hope to work together soon.

title: Transaction Coordinator / Administrative Assistant - land investing
description:
BT LAND GROUP

Job Description



Position: Transaction Coordinator / Administrative Assistant

Reports To: Owner

Location: Remote (Virtual Assistant)

Schedule: Part-Time to Start (20–30 hrs/wk), Full-Time Potential

Probationary Period: 60 Days



About BT Land Group



BT Land Group is a real estate investment company specializing in the acquisition and resale of vacant land across most U.S. mainland states. We source off-market deals through data-driven outreach, purchase at a discount, take title, and list on the MLS for resale. As our deal volume grows, we need a detail-oriented, proactive person to keep the back end of our operation running flawlessly—from signed purchase agreement to closed deal, and all the administrative work that keeps the machine moving in between.



Role Overview



The Transaction Coordinator / Admin is the backbone of BT Land Group's operations. Once our Acquisitions Manager gets a purchase agreement signed, you take the deal from there—coordinating with title companies, managing escrow timelines, tracking documents, and ensuring every deal crosses the closing table on schedule. On the administrative side, you'll also be responsible for pulling county data and running skip traces to feed our marketing pipeline, maintaining CRM hygiene, and supporting the team with day-to-day operational tasks. This hybrid role requires someone who is extremely organized, detail-obsessed, and comfortable managing multiple priorities across different stages of the business at once.



Key Responsibilities



Transaction Coordination (Post-Contract to Close)



- Manage the full closing process from signed purchase agreement through to successful closing: coordinate with title companies, real estate attorneys, and escrow agents across multiple states.

- Order title searches, review title commitments, and proactively flag any issues (liens, encumbrances, boundary disputes, back taxes) for resolution before they delay closing.

- Prepare, send, track, and organize all closing documents—purchase agreements, addendums, assignments, deeds, settlement statements, and any required disclosures.

- Serve as the primary point of contact for sellers during the closing process: keep them informed on timeline, answer questions, and resolve concerns promptly and professionally.

- Maintain and monitor closing timelines and critical deadlines for every active deal—ensure nothing slips through the cracks.

- Coordinate wire transfers and funding logistics with title/escrow and confirm successful recording and closing of each transaction.

- Maintain a real-time closing tracker in Stride CRM with current status, next steps, and any blockers for every deal in the pipeline.



Data, Skip Tracing, and Administrative Support



- Pull county data lists and property records from relevant sources to feed the SMS marketing and cold calling campaigns.

- Run skip tracing on property owner lists to obtain current, accurate phone numbers,
 addresses, and mailing addresses.

- Clean, deduplicate, organize, and upload data lists into Smarter Contacts and Stride CRM for the marketing and calling teams.

- Maintain CRM data integrity: update deal statuses, clean up duplicate or outdated records, and ensure information flows accurately across the pipeline.

- Handle general administrative tasks including
 management, document organization, scheduling, and file management in Google Drive.

- Support the team with ad-hoc operational needs as the business scales—this role will evolve as we grow.



Requirements



- Prior experience as a transaction coordinator, real estate administrative assistant, or virtual assistant in a real estate environment (required).

- Exceptional organizational skills and meticulous attention to detail—you catch errors before they become problems.

- Comfortable managing multiple active transactions and administrative workstreams simultaneously without losing track of deadlines.

- Strong written communication skills for professional correspondence with title companies, escrow agents, attorneys, sellers, and team members.

- Experience with data entry, list management, and skip tracing workflows (or demonstrated ability to learn quickly).

- Self-motivated and proactive—you anticipate what needs to happen next rather than waiting to be told.

- Reliable internet connection and availability during U.S. business hours for coordination with title companies, escrow, and sellers.



Preferred Qualifications



- Experience with land transactions specifically—understanding of deeds, title nuances, and rural property closings (not just residential houses).

- Familiarity with CRM systems (Stride, Podio, REsimpli, or similar) and deal pipeline management.

- Experience with skip tracing platforms and county-level property data sources.

- Familiarity with Land ID, Land Portal, or similar property research and due diligence tools.

- Prior experience coordinating with title companies and escrow agents across multiple states simultaneously.



Compensation & Benefits



- Competitive hourly compensation based on experience and skill level.

- Performance-based bonus potential tied to deals successfully closed on time, data quality and throughput, and overall operational efficiency.

- Starts part-time (20–30 hours/week) with a 60-day probationary period. Strong performers will have the opportunity to transition to full-time (40 hours/week).

- Training on BT Land Group's closing process, data workflows, CRM systems, and land investing fundamentals.

- Clear path for pay growth and expanded responsibilities as deal volume increases and the operation scales.



How to Apply



Interested candidates should submit their resume along with a brief message covering the following:



- Your relevant experience in transaction coordination, real estate admin, or virtual assistant work.

- Your experience with data management, skip tracing, or list building (if applicable).

- A recording of yourself on a call. If you don't have an existing recording, please send a short clip of yourself introducing yourself as if you were speaking to a potential seller for the first time.

- Your availability (hours per week and time zone).

- Your favorite food.



BT Land Group is an Equal Opportunity Employer.

title: Acquisitions Manager - land Investing
description:
BT LAND GROUP

Job Description



Position: Acquisitions Manager

Reports To: Owner

Location: Remote (Virtual Assistant)

Schedule: Part-Time to Start (20–30 hrs/wk), Full-Time Potential

Probationary Period: 60 Days



About BT Land Group



BT Land Group is a real estate investment company focused on acquiring and reselling vacant land across most U.S. mainland states. We source off-market deals through high-volume SMS campaigns, cold calling, and data-driven targeting, purchase at a discount, take title, and list on the MLS for immediate resale. Our pipeline generates a steady flow of qualified seller leads—and we need a skilled Acquisitions Manager who can turn those conversations into signed contracts.



Role Overview



The Acquisitions Manager is our closer and the primary revenue driver on the team. You'll take warm, pre-qualified leads that have been vetted by the Lead Manager and SMS Marketer, and convert them into signed purchase agreements. You'll handle Call 1s—where you anchor pricing, build rapport, gather remaining property and motivation details, and uncover the seller's target price—and Call 2s—where you present or refine your offer, overcome objections, negotiate final terms, and get the contract signed. You will handle follow up on fallout leads as well. You need to be confident, consultative, and skilled at reading people over the phone. This role has a direct, measurable impact on the company's bottom line.



Key Responsibilities



- Conduct Call 1s with qualified sellers: anchor pricing using proven frameworks, build rapport, gather remaining property and motivation details, and uncover the seller's target price to assess deal viability.

- Conduct Call 2s: present or refine offers, negotiate final terms, overcome objections, address seller concerns, and close deals by getting purchase agreements signed.

-Ability to reengage older offers

- Review all lead information, due diligence notes, and prior conversation history in Stride CRM before each call to be fully prepared and avoid redundant questions.

- Use Land ID, Land Portal, and supporting tools to verify property details, pull comps, and confirm deal viability during and between calls.

- Structure offers based on BT Land Group's buy box and pricing guidelines—factoring in market value, target spread, close timeline, acreage, and deal size.

- Work closely with the Lead Manager to ensure leads are properly qualified and all necessary information is captured before calls are scheduled.

- Update Stride CRM with detailed call outcomes, negotiation notes, offer details, and deal status after every seller interaction.

- Track personal KPIs including calls taken, offers made, contracts signed, and conversion rates; actively work to improve performance over time.

- Share feedback with leadership on lead quality trends, common seller objections, and market observations to help refine the overall acquisition strategy.



Requirements



- Proven experience in sales, negotiations, or acquisitions—ideally in real estate investing or land (required).

- Strong phone presence: confident, professional, consultative, and able to control the direction and pace of a negotiation call.

- Skilled in negotiation techniques such as anchoring, looping, motivational interviewing, or similar consultative selling approaches.

- Ability to quickly analyze deal economics: market value, offer price, spread, close timeline, and risk factors.

- Comfortable handling objections and guiding sellers through concerns about pricing, process, and timeline with empathy and professionalism.

- Highly organized—able to manage multiple active negotiations at different stages without letting details slip.

- Self-driven, competitive, and results-oriented; motivated by closing deals and exceeding targets.

- Reliable internet connection, a quiet and professional workspace, and availability during U.S. business hours for seller calls.



Preferred Qualifications



- Direct experience in land acquisitions, real estate wholesaling, or land flipping.

- Deep understanding of vacant land valuation: comps, zoning, buildability, road access, utilities, wetlands, flood zones, and slope/terrain analysis.

- Experience using CRM platforms (Stride is bonus) and deal pipeline tracking systems.

- Familiarity with Land ID, Land Portal, or similar property research and due diligence tools.

- Demonstrated track record of closing deals and exceeding acquisition or sales targets in a remote environment.



Compensation & Benefits



- Competitive hourly compensation based on experience and skill level.

- Performance-based bonus structure tied directly to contracts signed, deal volume, and acquisition quality.

- Starts part-time (20–30 hours/week) with a 60-day probationary period. Strong performers will have the opportunity to transition to full-time (40 hours/week).

- Training on BT Land Group's acquisition process, negotiation frameworks, pricing strategy, and deal structure.

- Significant opportunity for pay growth and expanded bonus potential as deal volume and responsibilities increase.



How to Apply



Interested candidates should submit their resume along with a brief message covering the following:



- Your relevant experience in sales, negotiations, or real estate acquisitions.

- A brief example of a deal you've closed or a negotiation you're proud of.

- A recording of yourself on a call. If you don't have an existing recording, please send a short clip of yourself introducing yourself as if you were speaking to a potential seller for the first time.

- Your availability (hours per week and time zone).

- Your favorite food.



BT Land Group is an Equal Opportunity Employer.

title: Nonprofit Grant Writer & Proposal Assistant
description:
I’m looking for a part-time Nonprofit Grant Writer & Proposal Assistant to support grant writing and proposal development for my nonprofit clients.



This role is ideal for someone who is strong in grant writing, proposal development, and written communication, and who can also support related tasks such as reviewing guidelines, drafting reports, adapting content for different funders, and keeping proposal materials organized and on track.



The primary focus of this role is helping move grant applications forward from outline to draft to submission support. I’m looking for someone who can work independently with background materials, previous proposals, funder guidelines, and strategic direction, without needing step-by-step instruction for every task.



About the Business

I run a fundraising consulting business that supports nonprofit organizations with strategy, campaigns, and systems. A key part of my work includes helping clients develop strong grant applications and manage the writing process in a clear, organized, and professional way.



I’m looking for someone who can support that work with strong writing, editing, attention to detail, and a willingness to work within established systems and workflows.



What You’ll Be Doing



 - Review funder guidelines and summarize key application requirements, questions, and deadlines

 - Draft grant proposals, letters of inquiry, reports, and other funder-facing content using source materials I provide

 - Adapt existing content for different funders and application formats

 - Pull key information from client documents, websites, reports, previous proposals, and approved source materials

 - Help organize proposal materials, supporting documents, and standard content

 - Maintain grant trackers, calendars, and application status updates

 - Edit and revise draft content based on feedback

 - Flag missing information, inconsistencies, or questions before submission

 - Support a clear and organized workflow across multiple deadlines and client projects



What Matters Most

Strong writing and editing skills are the most important requirements for this role.



A key aspect of our process is working within an AI-supported writing workflow. Our process uses AI in the preliminary drafting stage, followed by human review, refinement, and adaptation. I’m looking for someone who can use AI efficiently to support early drafts, then apply strong judgment, editing skills, and attention to detail to ensure the final writing is accurate, clear, funder-aligned, and reflective of the client’s voice, program focus, and approved source materials.



Strong written English is essential. Attention to detail is also very important, especially when working across different deadlines, applications, and supporting documents.



Experience with nonprofit grant writing, proposal support, or donor communications is a strong asset.



This Role Is a Good Fit If You:



 - Are confident in your writing, editing, and proofreading skills

 - Can review funder guidelines and pull out the most important details

 - Are comfortable turning background information into strong draft content

 - Can adapt writing for different funders, formats, and audiences

 - Are comfortable using AI as part of a structured writing process

 - Need minimal direction once you understand the goals and workflow

 - Are highly organized and able to manage details across multiple projects

 - Are comfortable working within project management systems like ClickUp and following established workflow templates

 - Catch small mistakes, inconsistencies, and missing pieces before submitting work

 - Communicate clearly and ask thoughtful questions when needed

 - Are interested in a longer-term working relationship



Qualities Needed to Succeed



Integrity

You communicate honestly, take responsibility for your work, and let me know if something is unclear or delayed.



Commitment

I’m looking for someone interested in building a longer-term working relationship, not just completing one quick task.



Desire to Learn

I value someone who is open to feedback, willing to improve, and interested in growing with the role over time.



Attention to Detail

You notice inconsistencies, missing information, formatting issues, and small mistakes before work is submitted.



Good Judgment

You know how to use AI as a support tool, not a substitute for thinking. You review outputs carefully, verify details, and make sure the final work is accurate and appropriate for the client and funder.



About the Role

This is a part-time role to start, with potential to grow over time depending on fit, workflow, and evolving needs.



This role may involve supporting multiple nonprofit clients and projects at the same time, so strong organization and comfort switching between priorities will be important.



To Apply

Use cccVA02 in your subject line. Please begin the first line of your
 application with the words east coast grant.



Please also include:



 - A short note about your experience with grant writing, proposal development, or proposal support

 - One or two writing samples related to grants, proposals, reports, or nonprofit writing

 - A brief note explaining your role in each sample

 - A short description of your process for turning source material into a strong draft

 - A note about your experience using AI to support writing, editing, or proposal development



This is a part-time role to start, with an estimated workload of approximately 15 hours per week. Hours may vary slightly depending on active deadlines and client needs. There is potential for this role to grow over time depending on fit, workflow, and evolving needs.

title: YouTube Video Editor
description:
**Title:** Video Editor — Pop Culture / Anime / Movies YouTube Channel (Long-Form & Short-Form)



**About Us:**



CM Studios is a growing YouTube channel focused on pop culture content — movies, anime, cartoons, TV shows, Star Wars, Marvel, DC, horror, and collectibles. We create both long-form YouTube videos and daily short-form content (Shorts, Reels, TikToks) and are looking for a skilled video editor to join our team and help us scale. We plan for this role to grow as the channel grows.



**What You'll Be Doing:**



- Editing long-form YouTube videos (our priority — at least one quality video per month)

- Editing daily short-form videos (15-60 seconds) for YouTube Shorts, TikTok, and Instagram Reels

- You will receive raw recorded footage and creative direction fro
 — I record all content and will communicate the vision for each video. You can also review the existing channel to understand the style and direction.

- You should have some relative knowledge of the niche. You don't have to be an expert — I am the expert. You can ask questions where needed, but I do expect you to be able to find quality clips, quality SFX, and quality level clips on your own.

- After each video is uploaded, spending approximately 1 hour on community engagement: responding to comments on our content and engaging with related creators' content to boost visibility

- Maintaining a consistent upload schedule and being available during upload windows



**What We're Looking For:**



- Proven experience editing both long-form and short-form content for YouTube

- Must have edited at least one short-form video that reached **1,000,000+ views on YouTube Shorts** or **10,000,000+ views on TikTok** (please include link)

- Must have edited at least one long-form video that reached **100,000+ views on YouTube** (please include link)

- Strong skills in storytelling, pacing, sound design, transitions, and retention editing for long-form YouTube content

- Strong understanding of hooks, text overlays, and trending editing styles for short-form platforms

- Familiarity with pop culture content — movies, anime (Dragon Ball, Naruto, One Piece, etc.), cartoons, TV shows, Star Wars, Marvel/DC, and horror

- Understanding of YouTube algorithm and what makes both long-form and short-form content perform

- Reliable internet connection and ability to communicate clearly in English

- Experience with community engagement and understanding of engagement strategies is a plus



**How You'll Be Paid:**



- **Short-form videos:** Per-video rate, hourly rate, or whatever pricing structure works best for you — just tell us how you prefer to charge for short-form edits

- **Long-form videos:** Hourly rate or per-project rate — tell us your preferred pricing structure

- **Engagement work:** 1 hour of community engagement paid after each upload

- Payment issued upon completion of each video cycle (edit + engagement hour)

- Consistent, ongoing work — this role is designed to grow as the channel grows



**To Apply, Please Include:**



1. A brief introduction about yourself and your editing experience

2. A link to at least one short-form video you edited that reached 1M+ views on YouTube Shorts or 10M+ on TikTok

3. A link to at least one long-form video you edited that reached 100K+ views on YouTube

4. Links to your portfolio or channels you've edited for, showing both long-form and short-form work

5. Your pricing: how you charge for short-form edits and your hourly or per-project rate for long-form

6. Review our content across all platforms (links below) and answer this question: **Pick one of our existing videos (long-form or short-form) and explain specifically how you would have edited it differently to get more views.** Be specific — mention the hook, pacing, storytelling, retention, transitions, or any changes you'd make.



**Our Channels (please review before applying):**

- YouTube: 

- TikTok: 

- Instagram: 

- Letterboxd: 



**Trial Period:**



Selected candidates will be given a paid trial — editing one 15-20 second test video to evaluate fit, style, and quality before moving forward.



**Important:** To confirm you've read this entire post carefully, please include the phrase **"the iron giant"** somewhere in your response.

title: Anti-Bot & Browser Fingerprinting Specialist — Web Automation (Remote)
description:
ABOUT US:

VisaBridge is a licensed travel agency specializing in Portuguese visa appointment automation. We currently operate a fully functional automation system (Python + Playwright) that processes
appointments every Friday. Our code works, but the embassy's anti-bot detection is constantly evolving. We need a specialist who can keep our system INVISIBLE to server-side detection — permanently.



THE REALITY:

Every Friday at 11:40 AM Lisbon time,
appointment slots are released and fully booked within 1-5 MINUTES. Our competitors use elite-level automation with advanced anti-detection techniques. The embassy is actively investing in bot detection technology. Any system that is identified as a bot gets blocked immediately — resulting in zero appointments for that session. This is a constant arms race, and we intend to WIN it.



YOUR MISSION:

You will analyze the embassy's anti-bot detection mechanisms and ensure our automation system passes ALL detection checks. You will also be on standby during booking windows to deploy countermeasures in real-time if the embassy updates their detection.



WHAT YOU WILL DO:

• Analyze the target server's bot detection — identify exactly what checks are performed (fingerprinting, header analysis, TLS fingerprint, behavioral analysis, rate limiting)

• Implement browser fingerprint spoofing — Canvas, WebGL, AudioContext, fonts, hardware concurrency, screen resolution, plugins, navigator properties

• Manipulate TLS fingerprints (JA3/JA4) to match genuine Chrome browser traffic

• Build dynamic header injection — headers must vary realistically per request, not be static

• Implement cookie/session persistence across booking cycles to reduce login frequency

• Build User-Agent rotation with CONSISTENT header sets (UA + sec-ch-ua + Accept + Accept-Language must all match)

• Monitor embassy website changes and deploy detection countermeasures BEFORE competitors adapt

• Stay on standby every Friday during booking window (6:50 PM - 9:00 PM PH time) to deploy emergency fixes



CRITICAL REQUIREMENT:

The embassy website and anti-bot systems change WITHOUT NOTICE. You MUST be available to analyze changes and deploy fixes IMMEDIATELY — especially on Fridays during the booking window. A 30-minute delay means losing thousands of euros. This is non-negotiable.



REQUIREMENTS:

• 4+ years experience in web scraping and browser automation

• Deep expertise in browser fingerprinting techniques

• Experience with TLS fingerprint manipulation (JA3/JA4)

• Strong knowledge of HTTP protocols, headers, and browser behavior

• Experience with network analysis tools (Wireshark, Charles Proxy)

• Python + Playwright advanced level

• Experience bypassing bot detection systems (Cloudflare, DataDome, PerimeterX, or similar)

• Ability to reverse-engineer anti-bot mechanisms from network traffic analysis

• Fast turnaround on detection countermeasures

• Stable internet connection (minimum 20 Mbps)



COMPENSATION:

• $1,200 - $2,500/month depending on experience level

• Performance bonus: $
/month based on detection evasion success rate

• Long-term position — this is an ongoing arms race, not a one-time project



HOW TO APPLY:

Send your resume along with:

1. Description of bot detection systems you have bypassed (with specific techniques used)

2. Your approach to browser fingerprint spoofing (tools, techniques, results)

3. How you would analyze a server's detection mechanism from scratch

4. Your available start date (earlier = better — competition is getting stronger every week)

title: Sales Closer — Help US Small Businesses Get Online ($10/hr + $50 Per Sale)
description:
About Us:                                                                                                                                             

  We're Typesite — we use AI to build custom websites for small businesses in under 30 minutes. Our product is live, we have paying customers, and we need someone to help us grow by reaching business owners who don't have a website yet.                                                                

                                                                                                                                                        

  The Role:

  You'll be calling US-based small business owners (landscapers, contractors, cleaning companies, etc.) who we've already identified as not having a website. This is a two-call process:                                                                                                                  

   

  Call 1 — The Easy Call:                                                                                                                               

  You offer to build them a free custom website. No catch, no credit card. You ask about their business, collect some basic info, and we build their site using AI in about 30 minutes.                                                                                                                    

   

  Call 2 — The Close:                                                                                                                                   

  You call them back, walk them through their finished website, and close them on a $50/month hosting subscription. They're looking at a real site with their business name, photos, and services already on it. You're not selling an idea — you're showing them something that's already built for them.    

   

  Compensation:                                                                                                                                         

  - $8-10/hr base (depending on experience)                       

  - $50 commission for every new paying subscriber you close                                                                                            

  - Top performers can realistically earn $3,000-4,000+ per month

                                                                                                                                                        

  Schedule:                                                                                                                                             

  - 30 hours/week (flexible within US business hours, roughly 8am-5pm EST)

  - Long-term ongoing role, not a short project                                                                                                         

                                                                  

  What We Provide:                                                                                                                                      

  - Pre-built lead lists of businesses without websites (you're not hunting for leads)

  - A product that builds itself in 30 minutes (you don't need to know anything technical)                                                              

  - A free offer for the first call that makes the conversation easy                      

  - Training on the product and the close                                                                                                               

                                                                                                                                                        

  You're a Great Fit If You:                                                                                                                            

  - Have experience calling US-based customers and are comfortable with American conversational style                                                   

  - Sound natural and warm on the phone — not robotic or scripted                                                                                       

  - Have done cold calling, appointment setting, or phone sales before                                                                                  

  - Can manage your own callback schedule without being micromanaged                                                                                    

  - Speak clear, confident English                                                                                                                      

  - Get excited about commission because you know you can close                                                                                         

                                                                                                                                                        

  How to Apply:                                                                                                                                         

  Record a 60-second voice note introducing yourself as if you're calling a landscaping business owner to offer them a free website. Don't read a script

   — just be yourself. Send it with your application. If I enjoy the conversation, we'll talk.

title: Credit Repair Sales Agent
description:
We are seeking an experienced Credit Repair Sales Agent whose primary responsibility is to convert leads into enrolled clients.



This is a sales-only position. You will not be handling disputes, backend processing, or account servicing.



Your role is to speak with inbound leads, identify their needs, present our services, and close them into our program. This position requires urgency, confidence, and the ability to consistently convert prospects into paying clients.



Key Responsibilities

 • Contact new leads immediately as they enter the pipeline

 • Conduct consultation calls to assess the prospect’s credit situation

 • Lead enrollment calls and guide clients into the program

 • Follow up with leads through calls, SMS, and CRM communication

 • Handle objections and confidently ask for the sale

 • Accurately document all interactions in GoHighLevel CRM

 • Maintain consistent communication with management



Compensation

 • Hourly pay based on experience

 • Commission per enrollment

 • Performance-based bonuses available



This is a performance-driven role with strong earning potential for the right candidate.

Minimum Requirements (Do Not Apply If You Do Not Meet These)

 • At least 1 year of credit repair experience

 • At least 2 years of sales experience (phone sales preferred)

 • Strong understanding of:

 • Credit reports

 • Collections, charge-offs, and late payments

 • Credit-building strategies

 • Excellent English communication skills (verbal and written)

 • Reliable internet connection and quiet work environment

 • Ability to work U.S. business hours

Ideal Candidate



The ideal candidate is:

 • Highly responsive and able to act quickly on new leads

 • Confident and in control of conversations

 • Results-driven and motivated by commission

 • Organized and detail-oriented

 • Professional in tone and communication

 • Consistent with follow-up and lead management

Performance Expectations

 • Contact leads within minutes of receiving them

 • Build rapport and clearly identify the prospect’s needs

 • Present the service in a structured and confident manner

 • Consistently convert leads into enrollments



Why Join Our Team

 • Consistent flow of inbound leads

 • Established brand with a strong reputation

 • Proven systems and structured sales process

 • Opportunity for growth and increased earnings

Important



This is not an entry-level position. We are looking for someone who already understands both credit repair and sales and can perform without constant supervision.

title: Social Media Manager, Podcast Manager, Executive Assistant
description:
Full-Time Virtual Assistant — Media & Content Operations

Company: The AI Report

Location: Remote (Philippines)

Hours: Full-time, 40 hours/week (Monday–Friday)

Salary: $1,000–$1,200 USD/month (based on experience)

Start Date: Immediately



About Us

The AI Report is one of the fastest-growing AI media companies in the world — 400,000+ newsletter subscribers, 61% open rate, two active podcasts, a YouTube channel, and a Circle community for executives. We're based in Montreal, Canada.



We're hiring a full-time VA to own our day-to-day content operations so our CEO can focus on growth, partnerships, and speaking engagements. This is a high-trust, high-responsibility role — not data entry.



Important: This role requires near-native English fluency — both written and spoken. You'll be writing public-facing content (show notes, YouTube descriptions, community posts) and communicating directly with the CEO daily via voice and video. If English isn't your strongest language, this isn't the right fit.



What You'll Do

Podcast & Audio Production (10–12 hrs/week)



Upload episodes to Spotify, Apple Podcasts, and YouTube



Generate and attach transcripts using AI tools



Write show notes and episode descriptions



Coordinate guest scheduling via Calendly



Create and schedule audiogram clips for social media



YouTube Management (6–8 hrs/week)



Upload videos with optimized titles, descriptions, tags, and thumbnails



Manage playlists and end screens



Monitor comments and flag anything needing a response



Track performance metrics weekly



Community Management (4–6 hrs/week)



Upload content to our Circle community



Monitor community engagement and respond to basic questions



Organize and tag community resources



Design Support (4–6 hrs/week)



Create social media graphics, thumbnails, and visual assets in Canva



Maintain brand consistency across all visual content



Resize and adapt creatives for different platforms



Admin & Operations (8–10 hrs/week)



Triage the CEO's inbox (Gmail)



Update contacts and deal stages in Notion CRM



Calendar management and meeting prep



Basic research tasks as needed



Maintain SOPs and checklists in Notion



Required Skills

Near-native English fluency — written and spoken. You'll write public-facing content and speak with the CEO daily. This is non-negotiable.



Experience with YouTube (uploading, metadata, thumbnails)



Experience with podcast hosting platforms (Spotify for Podcasters, Apple Podcasts Connect, RSS)



Experience using AI tools (Claude, ChatGPT, or similar) in your daily workflow



Comfortable working in Notion



Design skills — able to create and edit graphics in Canva or similar tools



Tech-savvy and fast learner — you'll also use Circle, Calendly, and Google Workspace



Self-starter who works independently, follows SOPs, and flags problems early



Reliable internet connection and quiet workspace



Nice-to-Have

Familiarity with the AI industry



Video editing experience



Experience supporting a CEO or founder directly



What Success Looks Like

Week 1: You've learned all our platforms, completed onboarding, and handled your first episode uploads and YouTube posts independently.



Month 1: You own the full podcast-to-publish pipeline. Episodes go live on time, every time. YouTube is updated same-day. Circle is active and organized. The CEO's inbox is clean.



Month 3: You're anticipating needs before being asked. You've improved our SOPs. You're flagging performance insights. The CEO spends zero time on operations.



Compensation & Growth

$1,000–$1,200 USD/month based on experience



Paid Philippine holidays



Performance review and raise opportunity at 90 days



Path to Content Operations Manager as the company scales



You'll work directly with the CEO — high-visibility role with real impact



How to Apply

Record a short video (2–3 minutes) introducing yourself, your experience, and why you're a good fit. Upload to YouTube (unlisted) or Loom and share the link.



Attach your resume or portfolio



Answer these questions in your application:



What is your experience with YouTube channel management? Give a specific example.



Have you worked with podcast hosting platforms before? Which ones?



Describe a time you managed multiple recurring tasks with tight deadlines. How did you stay organized?



What AI tools do you currently use, and how do you use them in your work?



What is your current internet speed? (Run a test at speedtest.net and share the result)

title: Google Ads Specialist (Service Businesses in Australia)
description:
We are currently looking for an experienced Google Ads Specialist to join our team and manage advertising campaigns for clients within the trades and service-based industries in Australia.



Our agency works primarily with businesses such as electricians, plumbers, builders, landscapers, and other local service providers, so we are specifically looking for someone who has previous experience running Google Ads campaigns for similar industries, ideally within the Australian market.



The role will involve setting up, managing, and optimizing Google Ads campaigns with the goal of generating high-quality leads and enquiries for local service businesses. We are looking for someone who understands how to structure campaigns effectively for service-based businesses, manage budgets efficiently, and continually improve campaign performance.



This is an opportunity for someone who is confident working independently, understands the local lead generation model for trades businesses, and can provide strategic insight into how campaigns should be structured and optimized.



Responsibilities:



• Set up and manage Google Ads campaigns for trades and service-based businesses

• Conduct keyword research focused on high-intent service searches

• Create and optimize search campaigns designed to generate leads and enquiries

• Monitor campaign performance and make ongoing optimizations

• Improve click-through rates, conversion rates, and lead quality

• Provide insights and recommendations to improve campaign performance

• Track and report campaign results where required



Requirements:



• Proven experience managing Google Ads campaigns

• Experience working with trades or service-based businesses (electricians, plumbers, builders, etc.)

• Ideally experience running Google Ads campaigns within the Australian market

• Strong understanding of local lead generation campaigns

• Ability to manage budgets and optimize campaigns for performance

• Strong analytical and problem-solving skills

• Reliable communication and ability to meet deadlines



To confirm that you have read the full job listing, please include the phrase “I listen to Dylan” somewhere in your application.



When applying, please include proof of your Google Ads experience, such as case studies, screenshots of campaign results, or examples of campaigns you have managed, particularly if they involve trades or service-based businesses in Australia.



We are looking for someone who can become a trusted part of our team and help manage and grow Google Ads campaigns for our clients.

title: Senior Python Developer — CAPTCHA Solving & Web Automation (Remote)
description:
ABOUT US:

VisaBridge is a licensed travel agency specializing in Portuguese visa appointment automation. We currently operate a fully functional automation system (Python + Playwright) that processes
appointments every Friday. We are NOT starting from zero — our code works. But we need to make it FASTER, SMARTER, and more COMPETITIVE.



THE REALITY:

Every Friday at 11:40 AM Lisbon time, the Portuguese Embassy releases
appointment slots. These slots are FULLY BOOKED within 1-5 MINUTES by competing bot operators from India, Pakistan, Serbia, Cape Verde, and Portugal. These competitors run ELITE-LEVEL automation systems. If you are not the fastest, you get NOTHING. We currently outperform them (200+ appointments per week vs their combined ~80), but the gap is closing. We need engineers who can push our system to the absolute limit.



YOUR MISSION:

You will take our EXISTING working codebase and make it significantly faster and more efficient. This is NOT a build-from-scratch project — you will optimize, improve, and innovate on a system that already delivers results.



WHAT YOU WILL DO:

• Optimize CAPTCHA solving speed — our current pipeline takes 30-45 seconds per solve. Target: 10-15 seconds

• Implement token pre-loading — solve CAPTCHAs BEFORE page load and inject tokens instantly

• Build real-time API ranking — automatically route to the fastest CAPTCHA service at any given 
nt

• Optimize Playwright form filling — reduce all human-like delays to minimum safe thresholds

• Implement parallel processing — solve next CAPTCHA while current form is being submitted

• Reduce browser launch time and page navigation overhead

• Build session persistence — reuse browser sessions across multiple booking cycles to avoid repeated logins

• Stay on standby every Friday during booking window (6:50 PM - 9:00 PM PH time) to deploy hotfixes in real-time



CRITICAL REQUIREMENT:

The embassy website changes frequently. You MUST be available to analyze changes and update the code IMMEDIATELY. A 1-hour delay on a Friday means lost appointments worth thousands of euros. This is non-negotiable.



REQUIREMENTS:

• 3+ years Python development experience

• Strong experience with Playwright or Puppeteer

• Experience with CAPTCHA solving services (Anti-Captcha, CapSolver, 2Captcha)

• Strong async programming skills (asyncio, multithreading, multiprocessing)

• Experience optimizing web automation for SPEED (not just functionality)

• Ability to read and quickly understand existing codebases

• Fast turnaround on bug fixes and code updates

• Stable internet connection (minimum 20 Mbps)

• Available for a PAID technical test before hiring



COMPENSATION:

• $800 - $1,500/month depending on experience level

• Performance bonus: $
/month based on measurable speed improvements

• Long-term position — this is an ongoing project, not a one-time job



HOW TO APPLY:

Send your resume along with:

1. Your experience with CAPTCHA solving APIs and optimization

2. The fastest web automation project you have built (with timing details)

3. Your approach to reducing form filling time while avoiding detection

4. Your available start date (earlier = better — we need to move FAST)

title: Cold Caller Needed $5 bonus per lead / $4.00 Per Hour Part time
description:
IMPORTANT APPLICATION GUIDELINE: SUBMISSION MUST INCLUDE A VOICE RECORDING OR MOCK CALL



Position Available: Real Estate Investment Cold Caller

Role Summary



We are actively seeking a driven Real Estate Investment Sales Associate to join our dynamic team. This crucial position involves reaching out to property owners to discuss potential selling opportunities, assessing the viability of leads for our investment initiatives, and ensuring accurate handling of these leads within our CRM system, Podio. Candidates ideal for this role should possess a thorough understanding of the real estate investment landscape, outstanding telephone communication skills, and the ability to quickly establish rapport with potential clients.



Key Responsibilities



- Lead outbound calling campaigns to homeowners to scout for potential sales leads.

- Efficiently qualify leads, capturing key data for our CRM (Podio).

- Demonstrate a solid grasp of investment strategies in the real estate sector.

- Maintain a positive and resilient attitude, especially in the face of rejection.

- Communicate effectively and clearly in English.

- Prioritize productivity, ensuring the quality of interactions remains high.

- Work collaboratively with the team to achieve and exceed sales goals.



Application Submission Requirements



Please send the following to 
 to apply:

1. Your most recent resume.

2. Your Zoom ID for potential interview discussions.

3. A personal introduction video (1-2 minutes), detailing your real estate experience.

4. A voice recording or mock call to exhibit your telecommunication skills and ability to engage with potential clients.

*Operating Hours



- Monday to Friday, 10:00 AM to 2:00 PM Eastern Standard Time.



Compensation Package



- Guaranteed Base Pay: $80 per week.

- Hourly Rate: $4

- Dynamic Pay Structure: A bonus of $5 for each lead beyond the weekly minimum of 15 qualified leads max lead count 23 that qualify for bonus. This incentive aims to motivate and reward exceptional performance.



Required Qualifications



Virtual Assistance



- A stable internet connection, phone, USB headset, and computer are essential.

- Proficiency in CRM data management (e.g., Xencall).

- Experience with cold calling.

- Strong interpersonal skills and a friendly personality.

- Effective research skills.



Language Proficiency



- Fluent in English, both orally and in writing.



Marketing & Sales Experience



- A background in lead generation.

- Ability to conduct proactive outbound calls to prospective clients.



Payment and Bonuses



- Weekly payment with a base salary of $80.

- A $5 bonus for each lead generated over the weekly minimum of 15, establishing a dynamic and rewarding pay model.

- The position entails 4 hours of cold calling each day.

- Availability for a 5-day workweek is required.

- Engagement is expected for four weeks per month, with compensation directly linked to your success in generating deals.

We are actively seeking a driven Real Estate Investment Sales Associate to join our dynamic team. This crucial position involves reaching out to property owners to discuss potential selling opportunities, assessing the viability of leads for our investment initiatives, and ensuring accurate handling of these leads within our CRM system, Podio. Candidates ideal for this role should possess a thorough understanding of the real estate investment landscape, outstanding telephone communication skills, and the ability to quickly establish rapport with potential clients.



Key Responsibilities



- Lead outbound calling campaigns to homeowners to scout for potential sales leads.

- Efficiently qualify leads, capturing key data for our CRM (Podio).

- Demonstrate a solid grasp of investment strategies in the real estate sector.

- Maintain a positive and resilient attitude, especially in the face of rejection.

- Communicate effectively and clearly in English.

- Prioritize productivity, ensuring the quality of interactions remains high.

- Work collaboratively with the team to achieve and exceed sales goals.



Application Submission Requirements



Please send the following to 
 to apply:

1. Your most recent resume.

2. Your Zoom ID for potential interview discussions.

3. A personal introduction video (1-2 minutes), detailing your real estate experience.

4. A voice recording or mock call to exhibit your telecommunication skills and ability to engage with potential clients.

*Operating Hours



- Monday to Friday, 10:00 AM to 2:00 PM Eastern Standard Time.



Compensation Package



- Guaranteed Base Pay: $80 per week.

- Hourly Rate: $4

- Dynamic Pay Structure: A bonus of $5 for each lead beyond the weekly minimum of 15 qualified leads max lead count 23 that qualify for bonus. This incentive aims to motivate and reward exceptional performance.



Required Qualifications



Virtual Assistance



- A stable internet connection, phone, USB headset, and computer are essential.

- Proficiency in CRM data management (e.g., Xencall).

- Experience with cold calling.

- Strong interpersonal skills and a friendly personality.

- Effective research skills.



Language Proficiency



- Fluent in English, both orally and in writing.



Marketing & Sales Experience



- A background in lead generation.

- Ability to conduct proactive outbound calls to prospective clients.



Payment and Bonuses



- Weekly payment with a base salary of $80.

- A $5 bonus for each lead generated over the weekly minimum of 15, establishing a dynamic and rewarding pay model.

- The position entails 4 hours of cold calling each day.

- Availability for a 5-day workweek is required.

- Engagement is expected for four weeks per month, with compensation directly linked to your success in generating deals.

title: SEO Specialist (Australian Businesses – Ongoing Work)
description:
We are currently looking for an experienced and reliable SEO Specialist to join our team and assist with ongoing SEO campaigns for clients across Australia.



Our agency works primarily with trades and service-based businesses such as electricians, plumbers, builders, landscapers, and other local service providers. Because of this, we are specifically looking for someone who has previous experience working with Australian businesses, understands local SEO, and knows how to generate real, measurable results.



This role will involve both strategy and execution, with a strong focus on local SEO performance, content, and ongoing optimisation. There is consistent ongoing work available, and we are ideally looking for someone who can become a long-term part of our team.



Responsibilities:



• Conduct SEO audits and identify opportunities for improvement

• Perform keyword research focused on local Australian search intent

• Implement on-page SEO optimisations (meta titles, descriptions, headings, internal linking, etc.)

• Write and publish SEO-optimised blog content 

• Assist with Google Business Profile (GBP) management including posts, updates, and optimisation (training can be provided)

• Build local citations and directory listings to improve authority and NAP consistency

• Support location page creation and expansion targeting specific suburbs and service areas

• Carry out basic technical SEO checks (Search Console, errors, page speed, etc.)

• Provide monthly reporting including rankings, traffic insights, and work completed

• Work alongside our team to continuously improve campaign performance



Requirements:



• Proven experience in SEO

• Experience working with Australian businesses (preferably trades or service-based industries)

• Strong understanding of local SEO and Google rankings

• Ability to write or assist with SEO content (blogs, service pages, etc.)

• Good written and spoken English communication skills

• Experience with tools such as Google Search Console, Google Analytics, and SEO platforms

• Strong attention to detail and ability to follow structured processes

• Ability to show proof of results (rankings, traffic growth, lead generation, etc.)



To confirm that you have read the full job listing, please include the phrase “I listen to Dylan” somewhere in your application.



When applying, please include examples of SEO work you have completed, particularly for Australian businesses, along with any results, case studies, or data you can share.



We are looking for someone who can become a reliable and results-driven part of our team, helping us grow rankings, traffic, and leads for our clients through consistent and effective SEO work.

title: Cold Email & LinkedIn Outreach Specialist (Smartlead, Heyreach, Zapmail)
description:
Algominds is a London-based AI sales company hiring a Cold
 & LinkedIn Outreach Specialist based in the Philippines to manage and optimize outreach campaigns across our client accounts.



This is a hands-on execution role. You will be building, managing, and optimizing cold
 and LinkedIn outreach campaigns daily using specific tools. This is not a general VA role.



YOU MUST HAVE DIRECT EXPERIENCE WITH THESE THREE PLATFORMS:



1. Smartlead.ai — campaign setup, mailbox rotation, lead management, warmup settings, master inbox management

2. 
 — LinkedIn automation, multi-sender campaigns, connection requests, messaging sequences

3. Zapmail.ai — domain setup, mailbox provisioning, DNS configuration, deliverability management



If you have not used all three of these tools, please do not apply. We will verify your experience on our onboarding call.



WHAT THE ROLE INVOLVES:



Build and launch cold
 campaigns in Smartlead across multiple client workspaces. Set up and manage LinkedIn outreach sequences in Heyreach. Provision and maintain
 infrastructure including domains, mailboxes, and DNS records in Zapmail. Monitor deliverability metrics, bounce rates, and warmup health daily. Manage lead uploads, list segmentation, and campaign targeting. Respond to and categorize replies in Smartlead master inbox. A/B test subject lines, copy, and sending schedules to improve open and reply rates. Report weekly on campaign performance across all client accounts. Coordinate with our London-based team via Slack.



COMPENSATION:



$300 per month base salary paid via Wise on the 1st of every month. Performance bonuses based on campaign metrics including reply rates and meetings booked. Top performers managing multiple client accounts earn $700 to $1,000+ per month. No cap on bonuses.



REQUIREMENTS:



Based in the Philippines. Proven hands-on experience with Smartlead.ai, 
, and Zapmail.ai. This is non-negotiable. Understanding of
 deliverability including SPF, DKIM, DMARC, domain reputation, and inbox placement. Experience writing or editing cold
 copy and LinkedIn messaging sequences. Comfortable managing multiple client campaigns simultaneously. Excellent written English. Reliable internet connection and laptop or PC. Wise account or willingness to create one. Available during overlapping hours with UK timezone for weekly check-ins.



PREFERRED BUT NOT REQUIRED:



Experience with Clay, Instantly, Lemlist, or similar outreach tools. Familiarity with LinkedIn Sales Navigator. Experience working in an agency environment managing multiple client accounts. Understanding of B2B lead generation and ICP targeting.



IN YOUR APPLICATION PLEASE INCLUDE:



1. Which of the three tools Smartlead, Heyreach, and Zapmail you have the most experience with and for how long

2. A brief example of campaign results you have achieved such as open rates, reply rates, or meetings booked

3. How many client accounts or campaigns you have managed at once



Generic applications that do not address these three points will not be reviewed.

title: Compliance Researcher Virtual Assistant (Part-time / Full-time Remote)
description:
About the Company



We are an early-stage SaaS startup building ComplianceOS — an intelligent compliance operating system for U.S. construction contractors and trades businesses. Our platform helps contractors track licenses, permits, insurance, and certifications so they avoid costly fines, project shutdowns, and lost bids.



We are currently in the MVP stage and need help building the core requirements database that powers our gap analysis engine.



Note: create a video to have an intro about yourself, your outstanding works in the past (with quantitative data) and why you are the best fit for this position?



# Job Summary

We are looking for a smart, organized, and detail-oriented Compliance Researcher VA to help map licensing, permit, and compliance requirements for California general contractors and trades (with plans to expand later).



You will use official government websites, PDFs, and AI tools to research and structure data into clean checklists. Strong attention to detail and comfort with AI tools are more important than deep prior experience.



# Key Responsibilities



- Research and document licensing/permit requirements for California Contractors State License Board (CSLB) classifications, county business licenses, workers’ compensation, general liability insurance, bonds, and related items.

- Build and maintain structured spreadsheets or database matrices (e.g., “For a General Contractor in Los Angeles doing residential remodeling ? list of 10–15 required items with expiry rules, links, and notes”).

- Review official sources (CSLB guides, county portals, state websites) and summarize rules clearly.

- Use AI tools (Claude, ChatGPT, Perplexity, etc.) to extract information from long PDFs and draft initial checklists — then verify accuracy against original sources.

- Flag ambiguous or conflicting information and escalate to the founder for review.

- Keep the requirements database up-to-date as rules change (monitor key portals monthly).

- Organize research findings in Notion, Airtable, or Google Sheets for easy use by the development team.



Requirements / What We’re Looking For



- Some experience in legal research, paralegal work, administrative compliance, or regulatory document review (even 6–12 months is helpful).

- Excellent English reading and writing skills — you must be able to summarize complex rules into simple, clear language.

- Strong attention to detail and organizational skills (this role involves a lot of data accuracy).

- Comfortable using AI tools daily to speed up research (we will share effective prompts).

- Self-motivated and able to work independently with minimal supervision.

- Basic familiarity with U.S. government websites or licensing boards is a big plus (but not required — we will train you).

- Reliable internet connection and quiet workspace.



Nice-to-have (not required):

- Previous experience as a legal VA, paralegal assistant, or compliance support role.

- Experience working with construction, contractor licensing, or regulated industries.



# What We Offer



- Monthly compensation: $600–$800 (depending on experience and output quality).

- Flexible remote work (Philippines or similar time zones preferred for overlap with U.S. founder).

- Opportunity to grow with a fast-moving startup (potential for more hours and higher pay as the company scales).

- Clear weekly goals, templates, and direct guidance from the founder.

- Performance bonuses for high-quality, timely deliverables.



# How to Apply

Please reply with:

- Your resume or short bio highlighting any legal/research/compliance experience.

- Why you think you’d be a good fit for this role (2–3 sentences).

- A sample of any research or summarization work you’ve done (even a short example is fine).

title: Social Media & Community Manager (LinkedIn + Canva)
description:
Social Media & Community Manager

Female Lawyers' Club

Please note: applications must be submitted via the onlinejobs.ph messaging system only. Applications sent by
 will not be considered.

________________________________________

About Female Lawyers' Club

Female Lawyers' Club is a professional membership community for senior female lawyers in the UK and beyond. Through membership, online events and courses, we support women in the legal profession to build sustainable, well-paid and fulfilling careers.

Our members are experienced, senior professionals - partners, consultant solicitors, barristers and in-house counsel. The tone of everything we do reflects that: warm and human, but always professional.

The Founder, Rachel Spink, is a practising employment lawyer who runs Female Lawyers' Club alongside her legal work. This role is a key hire that will allow Rachel to focus on strategy, content and growing the business.

________________________________________

About the role

This is a part-time remote role starting at 5 hours per week, with the expectation that hours will grow as the business grows.

The main purpose of the role is to manage and grow Female Lawyers' Club's social media presence and 
munity, and to keep track of what is working so we can improve over time.

This is not a role for someone who simply wants to schedule posts. We are looking for someone who takes genuine ownership, thinks strategically about engagement, and understands how to communicate with a senior professional audience.

________________________________________

Responsibilities

Community engagement

• Managing and growing our LinkedIn group (hundreds of senior lawyers)

• Posting discussion prompts and conversation starters

• Welcoming new members and making them feel included

• Responding to comments professionally and warmly

• Supporting engagement in our members' WhatsApp group

• Sharing relevant articles and discussion topics

Social media content

• Turning blog posts and newsletters into LinkedIn and Instagram posts

• Creating LinkedIn carousels and graphics using Canva

• Scheduling 3–5 posts per week across platforms

• Repurposing existing content into new formats

• Suggesting new content ideas based on what is resonating with our audience

Performance tracking

• Keeping an ongoing record of key metrics: likes, comments, shares and engagement rates

• Tracking what types of content perform best and flagging patterns

• Making suggestions over time for how to improve performance based on the data

Design and content creation

• Creating social media graphics and carousel posts in Canva

• Maintaining consistency with the Female Lawyers' Club brand

• Occasionally creating simple PDFs, workbooks or event marketing materials

Website support (occasional)

• Uploading blog posts and updating website copy (Squarespace experience helpful but not essential)

________________________________________

What we are looking for

Essential:

• Excellent written English - this is the single most important requirement

• A professional, warm tone that is appropriate for senior lawyers

• Experience with LinkedIn, including LinkedIn groups

• Strong Canva skills with an eye for clean, professional design

• Ability to work independently and manage your own time without constant direction

• Reliable and responsive - you will be expected to communicate promptly

• Genuine interest in the mission of Female Lawyers' Club

Also valuable:

• Experience managing online communities

• Understanding of social media analytics and performance tracking

• Experience with Instagram

• Squarespace experience

________________________________________

Important note on tone

Our audience is made up of senior female lawyers - partners, barristers and experienced solicitors. The way we communicate with them must always be professional, warm and intelligent. It must never be overly casual, excitable or generic.

If you are used to writing for lifestyle, influencer or general consumer audiences, please consider carefully whether this role is the right fit. We are not looking for that style of content.

________________________________________

What it is like working with Rachel

• Rachel is kind, honest and wants you to succeed

• She is very busy, so she needs someone who can take real ownership of this role without needing constant direction

• She values reliability and clear communication above almost everything else

• Hours and pay can increase as the business grows

________________________________________

Hours and pay

• 5 hours per week to start

• Hours will increase as the business grows

• Rate is negotiable depending on experience

• Rachel is based in the UK so some overlap in working hours is needed

________________________________________

How to apply

Please apply via the onlinejobs.ph messaging system only. Do not send applications by
.

To be considered, please include all of the following in your message:

1. A brief introduction explaining why you are interested in this role and why you would be a good fit. Please keep this to a maximum of 150 words.

2. Answer this question: Below is a comment posted by a member inside a professional community for senior female lawyers. Please write a reply to this comment as if you were managing the community on behalf of the Founder.

"I've just made partner after twelve years. I honestly didn't think it would happen. I'm not sure whether to feel proud or just exhausted."

Your reply should be warm, genuine and professional. It should feel like it comes from a real person, not a corporate account. Maximum 5 sentences.

3. Share 2–3 examples of Canva graphics or social media posts you have created. A portfolio link is fine.

4. Please attach your CV or resume.

Please include the phrase "FLC April 2026" at the start of your message so that we know you have read the full description.

________________________________________

Applications that do not follow these instructions will not be considered.

title: Software Implementation Specialist
description:
# Software Implementation Specialist



**Location:** Remote (Philippines)

**Schedule:** US business hours, Eastern Time preferred

**Type:** Full-time



---



## About Company



We are a wealth management data infrastructure company. We help RIAs, broker-dealers, family offices, and TAMPs turn fragmented data into something they can actually use. We're a small, fast-moving team where your work is visible and your contributions matter.



---



## The Role



You'll be the bridge between our customers and our platform—the person who takes what a client needs and turns it into a working configuration.



This isn't a back-office role. You'll be on customer calls throughout the day, then in the platform making things happen. A typical day might start with a kickoff call for a new client, shift into configuring their data integrations, and end with troubleshooting an issue for an existing customer.



If you've worked at a development agency, digital consultancy, or software company where you balanced technical work with client interaction, you'll recognize the rhythm.



---



## What You'll Do



- Execute configuration work based on project plans and customer requests

- Join implementation calls, kickoffs, and status meetings

- Write SQL to query and validate data

- Modify HTML templates and work with JSON for integrations

- Document configurations and maintain project records

- Escalate to product and engineering when custom solutions are needed



---



## What We're Looking For



**Technical**

- Working knowledge of SQL—you can write queries, not just run them

- Comfortable reading and modifying HTML

- Experience with JSON in APIs, data structures, or config files



**Experience**

- 2+ years in a client-facing technical role at an agency, consultancy, or software company

- Track record of juggling multiple projects or clients at once

- Strong written and verbal English; comfortable on video calls with US clients



**Setup**

- Available during US Eastern business hours (roughly 8am–5pm ET)

- Reliable internet and a quiet space for calls

- Self-directed—you see a problem, you figure it out

You'll work directly with leadership and have real influence on how we serve customers. We're building something meaningful in an industry that's overdue for better technology, and we offer competitive compensation and a collaborative remote environment to match.



---

Send your resume with a few sentences about a time you had to balance technical work with customer communication. We read every application and respond to everyone.

title: WordPress/Elementor Website Designer
description:
We're an Australian web design agency looking for a reliable WordPress/Elementor designer to join our team on an ongoing basis.



We have existing website templates already built and you'll be responsible for bringing each site to life visually. This isn't a copy and paste job and you'll need to make design decisions to ensure each site looks clean, professional and polished. You'll always have an existing template as a reference so you're never starting from scratch.



Client images, logos and brand colours will be provided. All copywriting is handled by us so you only need to focus on the visual side.



What you'll be doing:



* Working from existing WordPress/Elementor templates

* Applying client visuals and making design decisions to bring each site to life

* Adjusting layouts, colour schemes and visual elements to match client branding

* Ensuring the finished product looks professional and consistent

* Delivering completed sites within 24-48 hours - this is a simple task and fast turnaround is a MUST



What we're looking for:



* Solid WordPress and Elementor experience - non negotiable

* A genuine eye for design - the finished product needs to look great every time

* Fast and reliable - we expect every project delivered within 24-48 hours without chasing

* Good communication - if something isn't clear ask rather than guess

* Able to work independently and make design decisions confidently



This is an ongoing role and we expect volume to grow significantly over the coming months. The right person will have consistent work long term and the chance to grow into a supervisor role down the line. 



To apply please start your message with "I listen Dylan" and include a portfolio of WordPress/Elementor websites you have built or worked on. 



Applications without a portfolio will not be considered.

title: Video Editor — Trading / Finance Long Form (10-20min)

title: eCommerce Video Editor
description:
We’re looking for a high-performance Video Editor to join our fast-growing pet supplement eCommerce brands.



This role is 100% focused on creating high-converting ads using AI tools. You’ll be responsible for turning creative briefs into finished ads across formats like VSL, UGC, and animation—at speed and scale.



This is not a traditional editing role. All creatives are AI-powered, so strong experience with AI tools is a must.



This is a full-time role (Monday–Friday) with consistent daily output expectations.



Who Are We?



We build high-growth ecommerce brands in the pet space, creating products that improve the health and happiness of dogs.



Our growth is driven by direct response marketing and high-volume creative output. We move fast, test aggressively, and scale what works.



Culture & Fit



We operate with a simple mindset:



- Speed > perfection

- Results > effort

- Constant iteration



This is a high-performance environment. If you want a slow, predictable 9–5, this isn’t it.



If you want to:



- Work directly with founders

- Produce work that directly impacts revenue

- Rapidly level up your skillset



You’ll fit right in.



Key Responsibilities:

- Turn creative briefs into high-converting video ads

- Produce VSL, UGC, and AI-generated ads

- Edit and iterate ads based on performance data

- Deliver 2+ completed briefs per day (minimum requirement)

- Collaborate with the team on creative improvements and new concepts

- Continuously optimize for CTR, CPA, and ROAS



Requirements:

- 3+ years experience editing for ecommerce / direct response

- Proven experience with:

- VSL ads

- UGC ads

- Animation-based creatives



Strong AI skillset (MANDATORY):

- Higgsfield

- HeyGen

- ElevenLabs



- Fluent English

- Strong understanding of performance-driven creatives

- Ability to work quickly and execute without constant oversight



Important:

We expect editors to use strong judgment and common sense when bringing briefs to life. Your first version should already be clean, on-brief, and conversion-focused—not something that requires heavy back-and-forth.



This is a high-output environment, so minimal revisions should be needed.



Preferred (not required):

- Experience with Slack

- Experience with Asana



Accountability Metrics:

- Minimum 2 completed ad briefs per day

- Consistent production of high-quality, testable creatives

- Low revision rates (high first-pass quality)

- Contribution to improving key ad metrics (CTR, CPA, ROAS)

- Speed, reliability, and execution



Compensation:

- $1,200 – $2,000/month (base)

- Performance-based bonuses tied to output and results



How We Hire

- Initial interview

- Test project

- Final interview (culture + performance fit)



We move fast and aim to hire within 7–10 days.



How to Apply



To be considered, please submit the following:

- 1–5 minute Loom video introducing yourself and walking through your experience with ecommerce ad creatives (show examples if possible)

- Your CV / Resume

Screening Questions
